dep.name: async-trait
dep.purpose: Async trait contracts for adapters, streams, and effect targets.

dep.name: clap
dep.purpose: CLI configuration for local client and remote server modes.

dep.name: futures-util
dep.purpose: Stream and sink helpers around websocket and async transport boundaries.

dep.name: http
dep.purpose: HTTP request and response primitives used by the WSS boundary.

dep.name: iroh
dep.purpose: Peer transport, NAT traversal, relay fallback, and encrypted connectivity primitives.

dep.name: libc
dep.purpose: Linux socket options, recvmsg control-message parsing, and original-destination recovery for the repo-local UDP helper surface.

dep.name: rustls-pemfile
dep.purpose: Load TLS certificate and private key material from PEM files.

dep.name: rustls-pki-types
dep.purpose: Typed certificate and key representations for rustls loaders.

dep.name: thiserror
dep.purpose: Deterministic project error enums across config, auth, transport, and proxy boundaries.

dep.name: tokio
dep.purpose: Async runtime for sockets, tasks, shutdown, timeouts, and backpressure handling.

dep.name: tokio-rustls
dep.purpose: TLS client and server integration on top of Tokio.

dep.name: tokio-util
dep.purpose: CancellationToken and TaskTracker helpers for shutdown and cleanup guarantees.

dep.name: tracing
dep.purpose: Structured logs and span-based diagnostic evidence.

dep.name: tracing-subscriber
dep.purpose: Structured log formatting and environment-based filtering.

dep.name: url
dep.purpose: Validated remote endpoint parsing for WSS configuration.

dep.name: x509-parser
dep.purpose: Certificate validation helpers such as expiry checks in TLS loading tests.

constraint.reason: Match the rust-version declared in Cargo.toml.
Anchor the first implementation to the currently published upstream surface.
Async runtime compatibility across websocket, proxy, and transport modules.

surface.name: telegram-transparent-helper
surface.decision: `Phase-37` freezes `redsocks` as the one exact experiment-local transparent interception helper class on the current Ubuntu 25.04 workstation.
surface.reason: `redsocks` is already available from the default Ubuntu package archive on this host, supports transparent TCP redirection plus UDP packets via SOCKS4/SOCKS5 or HTTP proxying, and does not require changing the existing n0wss runtime contract before synthetic helper smoke can begin.
surface.boundary: The helper stays experiment-local only, must remain reversible through explicit install and cleanup steps, and must not replace the preserved normal Desktop baseline at `127.0.0.1:1080`.

surface.name: telegram-arbitrary-udp
surface.decision: `Phase-40` freezes one exact arbitrary-destination UDP interception class for the current workstation: kernel-assisted interception with original-destination recovery plus one repo-local helper runtime.
surface.reason: On 2026-03-30 the host already exposed `nft`, `iptables`, and loaded kernel surfaces such as `nft_tproxy`, `xt_TPROXY`, `xt_socket`, `nf_tproxy_ipv4`, and `nf_tproxy_ipv6`, while no installable arbitrary-UDP helper binary such as `tun2socks`, `sing-box`, `gost`, or `hev-socks5-tunnel` was present. The existing `redsocks/redudp` path therefore remains only a fixed-destination smoke boundary, not the approved arbitrary-UDP class.
surface.boundary: The arbitrary-UDP surface must stay attached to the already logged-in ordinary Telegram Desktop baseline, must not introduce a second auth/bootstrap window, and must remain reversible through explicit experiment-local setup and cleanup without weakening the preserved `127.0.0.1:1080` path.

surface.name: telegram-nonredirect-live-topology
surface.decision: `Phase-43` freezes one exact non-REDIRECT live interception class for the current workstation: host-side Linux `TPROXY` for UDP plus the existing repo-local `origdst-live` helper.
surface.reason: The bounded Phase-42 live voice packet already proved live UDP activity, but the `iptables -t nat OUTPUT ... REDIRECT --to-ports 10073` topology collapsed recovered destination evidence to the helper listener itself. On this workstation the already-present kernel surfaces include `xt_TPROXY`, `xt_socket`, `nf_tproxy_ipv4`, and `nf_tproxy_ipv6`, so `TPROXY` is the narrowest honest next topology class that can preserve real destination semantics without introducing an external helper stack or reopening second-window auth branches.
surface.boundary: The non-REDIRECT live topology may change interception class only; the ordinary logged-in Telegram Desktop window, the governed `origdst-live` process shape, the preserved `127.0.0.1:1080` baseline, and the no-second-auth rule must remain fixed for the whole branch.

module-level.focus: Fast deterministic unit and contract checks around config, auth, and session logic.

wave-level.focus: Integration-lean checks for WSS handshake, session startup, timeout handling, and proxy request flow through the current bridge surface.

phase-level.focus: Broader transport, shutdown, and regression confidence.

Testing.mocking-policy: Prefer local fakes, loopback sockets, and deterministic transport doubles over broad mocks.

Observability.redaction: Never log secrets, auth tokens, proxy credentials, or raw proxied payload bytes.
Observability.tuning-note: Burst thresholds are operational defaults and are tuned after deployment using peak_rate_per_sec, rejection counters, and queue-capacity metrics documented in docs/OPERATORS.md.

DeliveryShape.shape: Source-first Rust repository with a library target, validated client or server CLI argument surfaces, GitHub release workflows, and deferred standalone binary packaging.
